jokes.text: Please, give us the land. It would be so nice.
jokes.setup: Leslie needs to compete with Gryzzl's $90 million offer
jokes.punchline: Her hilariously inadequate and overly polite pitch strategy
jokes.explanation: Pure Leslie Knope - earnest politeness in the face of impossible odds. The contrast between $90 million and 'it would be so nice' is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Whoa! Open your door?
jokes.setup: Mysterious notification arrives
jokes.punchline: Ben's confused/alarmed reaction to the ominous command
jokes.explanation: Sets up the escalating absurdity of Gryzzl's invasive technology. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, Ben! We're about to die. The robots have come for us. I made fun of you when you said it would happen but your novel has come true.
jokes.setup: Flying robot appears at their door
jokes.punchline: Leslie's immediate jump to apocalyptic conclusions and reference to Ben's sci-fi novel
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and character work. Leslie's dramatic overreaction combined with the callback to Ben's writing makes this memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: We're screwed.
jokes.setup: Realization that Gryzzl has flying robot delivery system
jokes.punchline: Leslie's defeated acceptance after her initial panic
jokes.explanation: Solid punctuation to the robot sequence. The economy is perfect - two words that capture her deflation.

jokes.text: What? You're done?
jokes.setup: Tom presents elaborate gourmet dish description
jokes.punchline: Andy's instant consumption despite the fancy presentation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom/Andy dynamic. Tom's foodie pretension meets Andy's simple appreciation. The timing must be perfect for this to land.

jokes.text: so I don't think about the marathon bone sesh they're probably having
jokes.setup: Tom explains why he's working on new dishes
jokes.punchline: Crude overshare about Lucy and Conrad's relationship
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom oversharing. The phrase 'marathon bone sesh' is memorably crude but feels slightly forced.

jokes.text: Yeah, but all I really do is goof around all day.
jokes.setup: Tom points out Andy only makes $100/week for his TV show
jokes.punchline: Andy's innocent dismissal of his actual extensive work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy - completely unaware of his value while doing everything. The irony lands because it's so believable for his character.

jokes.text: and his stupid crunches that he probably does
jokes.setup: Tom getting invested in Andy's contract to distract from Lucy
jokes.punchline: Specific, petty jealousy about Conrad's fitness routine
jokes.explanation: Excellent specificity. 'Stupid crunches' is perfect petty jealousy - Tom's imagining this guy's workout routine and resenting it.

jokes.text: Ow! Andy, my tum tum!
jokes.setup: Tom decides he should do crunches too
jokes.punchline: Immediate injury and childish complaint
jokes.explanation: Tom saying 'tum tum' is perfect character comedy. His immediate failure at exercise fits his vanity perfectly.

jokes.text: Honeybear and Sugar Plum are the special names Joe and I call each other. Yeah, we're adorable, but this is creepy.
jokes.setup: Donna explains why the GryzzlBox gifts are concerning
jokes.punchline: Self-aware acknowledgment of their cutesy nicknames followed by genuine concern
jokes.explanation: Great Donna moment - she owns her cutesy side but recognizes the privacy violation. The self-awareness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Biden the Rails: 1001 Poems Inspired by My Travels Through Amtrak's Northeast Corridor
jokes.setup: Leslie opens her GryzzlBox
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific fake book that perfectly matches Leslie's political interests
jokes.explanation: Brilliant fake book title. The specificity (1001 poems, Northeast Corridor) and Biden pun make this extremely quotable and perfectly Leslie.

jokes.text: A poster of the Supreme Court Justices sipping the Friends milkshake!
jokes.setup: Continuation of Leslie's perfect gifts
jokes.punchline: Bizarre mashup of Leslie's government obsession and pop culture
jokes.explanation: Absurd and perfect. The visual of Supreme Court justices sharing a milkshake is wonderfully weird and exactly what would appeal to Leslie.

jokes.text: You wanna put me on blast? I'ma put you on Front Street.
jokes.setup: Donna declares war on Gryzzl
jokes.punchline: Donna's street slang escalation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Donna voice - the escalating street terminology is both funny and intimidating. Great rhythm and character authenticity.

jokes.text: I don't understand what that means, but I think I get the context, and I love it.
jokes.setup: Donna's street slang declaration
jokes.punchline: Leslie's earnest confusion but enthusiastic support
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie - doesn't understand the cool terminology but fully supports the energy. Great character contrast with Donna.

jokes.text: Watermelon, martinis, exposed brick, Keri Russell's hair.
jokes.setup: April explains her anger management technique
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific list of random pleasant things
jokes.explanation: Perfectly April - the randomness of Keri Russell's hair alongside martinis is wonderfully specific and weird.

jokes.text: Those kids are me ten years ago, and this internship is the videotape from The Ring. It's too late for me. I've seen too much. But maybe I can save them.
jokes.setup: April reflecting on warning the interns
jokes.punchline: Horror movie metaphor for working at Parks department
jokes.explanation: Brilliant April monologue. The Ring metaphor is perfect - she's treating enthusiasm as a curse that spreads. Dark but self-aware.

jokes.text: They sent my husband three boxes of genital cream to his office. My husband's splotchy genitals are no one's business, except for his, mine, and his many genital doctors.
jokes.setup: Town meeting about Gryzzl privacy violations
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and uncomfortable medical overshare
jokes.explanation: Committed to the uncomfortable overshare. 'Many genital doctors' is the perfect escalating detail that makes it funnier and more awkward.

jokes.text: This is actually Ham-uel L. Jackson from the movie, Pork Fiction, it's extremely rare, and I am keeping it.
jokes.setup: Explanation of toy pig collection privacy violation
jokes.punchline: Elaborate pig movie star puns with possessive attachment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant pun work. Ham-uel L. Jackson and Pork Fiction are perfectly crafted, and the character's serious attachment makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Now Miley and Haley know I like to read. What if they tell Evan?
jokes.setup: Teen explaining Virginia Woolf books privacy violation
jokes.punchline: Treating literacy as shameful secret among peers
jokes.explanation: Smart teen stereotype inversion - being embarrassed about intellectual interests. The specific names add authenticity.

jokes.text: Actually, this is Tom Sell-oink, but, you know, close enough.
jokes.setup: Ron correcting Leslie about the pig figure
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact correction about celebrity pig pun name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - he knows exactly which celebrity pig this is and corrects it with complete seriousness. Tom Sell-oink is excellent.

jokes.text: Waterpark Vomit Chain Reaction
jokes.setup: Andy listing photo album highlights
jokes.punchline: Documenting disgusting family moment as cherished memory
jokes.explanation: Pure Andy - treating a disgusting incident as a precious family memory. The specific phrase is perfectly gross and memorable.

jokes.text: What, I'm just gonna carry around pictures of my child where anyone could see them?
jokes.setup: Ron immediately covering up photo of his son
jokes.punchline: Treating normal parental photo-sharing as security risk
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson paranoia. His approach to privacy extends even to normal family photos. Character integration is flawless.

jokes.text: frankly, if people are foolish enough to carry around a doodad that lets Gryzzl track their every move, this is what they get
jokes.setup: Leslie expects Ron to be outraged by privacy violations
jokes.punchline: Ron blaming the victims for using modern technology
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron - calling phones 'doodads' and having no sympathy for people who embrace technology. Completely in character.

jokes.text: keep it 100
jokes.setup: Ron defending Gryzzl's lawyers
jokes.punchline: Ron Swanson using current slang completely incorrectly
jokes.explanation: Hilarious contrast - Ron trying to use modern slang in formal context. The awkwardness is perfect for his character.

jokes.text: I've spent a fair amount of time with Gryzzl's high-priced lawyers, and they do seem to, as they say, 'keep it 100.'
jokes.setup: Ron defending Gryzzl's lawyers
jokes.punchline: Uses modern slang 'keep it 100' awkwardly and incorrectly
jokes.explanation: Ron trying to use modern slang is always funny - the careful quotation marks make it funnier

jokes.text: I'm Darlene Johannsen, and this is my assistant-turned-lover, Gregory Strong. Ours is a new romance, but one that sent shockwaves through my architecture firm.
jokes.setup: Leslie and Ben going undercover at Gryzzl
jokes.punchline: Leslie's unnecessarily dramatic fake backstory
jokes.explanation: Leslie can't help but create elaborate fiction even for simple undercover work. 'Sent shockwaves' is perfectly overwrought.

jokes.text: Ugh. Gross. What about you? Why are you here? The only thing I love more than parks is recreation.
jokes.setup: April asking intern about their passion for parks
jokes.punchline: Intern's overly earnest and obvious response
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff structure. The intern's earnestness contrasts nicely with April's cynicism.

jokes.text: Well, my mom's the devil and my dad's a dumb doctor.
jokes.setup: April asking about Jen's parents who made her do internship
jokes.punchline: Casual, devastating description of family
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery. The casual cruelty and alliteration make this memorable and funny.

jokes.text: It's like a chef at a restaurant, making all the food, and then the owner gets a profit-- which is my other job!
jokes.setup: Tom comparing being Andy's agent to restaurant ownership
jokes.punchline: Realizing he's describing his own exploitation of others
jokes.explanation: Great self-awareness moment. Tom accidentally exposes his own business model while defending his agent work. Perfect irony.

jokes.text: Pep Wiley, Chuck Warschank, Selena Gomez--I made them. You discovered Selena Gomez? Different Selena Gomez.
jokes.setup: Station manager bragging about discovering stars including Selena Gomez
jokes.punchline: Immediate deflation when pressed about the famous one
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation joke. The guy's obvious lie gets exposed immediately, and 'Different Selena Gomez' is perfectly pathetic.

jokes.text: What did you want to be when you were little? A scary mermaid that lures sailors to their death.
jokes.setup: April asking about childhood dreams
jokes.punchline: Jen's surprisingly dark and specific fantasy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor. The specificity of 'scary mermaid that lures sailors' is perfectly twisted and memorable.

jokes.text: I'm gonna file papers to adopt you as my child, so keep an eye out for that.
jokes.setup: April being impressed by Jen's darkness
jokes.punchline: Immediate decision to adopt this kindred spirit
jokes.explanation: Perfect April escalation. The casual tone about adoption papers makes it funnier and more absurd.

jokes.text: As you know, the cameras on your phones are always on, whether you're using them or not. I'm sorry, they are?
jokes.setup: Matty casually explaining invasive phone surveillance
jokes.punchline: Leslie's shocked reaction to this revelation
jokes.explanation: Good horror reveal structure. The casual delivery of terrifying information creates the right uncomfortable reaction.

jokes.text: So it's really just a coffee sales app. Yeah. Yeah. Yeah, we're developing with Starbucks.
jokes.setup: Elaborate explanation of mood-tracking technology
jokes.punchline: Simple admission it's just to sell coffee
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of tech-speak into corporate reality. The repeated 'yeah's show their embarrassment at being exposed.

jokes.text: to when you ladies need to score some new 'pons
jokes.setup: Roscoe listing what Gryzzl tracks
jokes.punchline: Awkward abbreviation for feminine products
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable tech-bro casualness about women's health. The slang makes it more awkward and cringey.

jokes.text: I mean, we just wanna learn everything about everyone and track them wherever they go and anticipate what they're about to do.
jokes.setup: Roscoe claiming there's nothing scary about Gryzzl
jokes.punchline: Describing exactly what makes them scary in casual terms
jokes.explanation: Excellent dramatic irony. Roscoe thinks he's being reassuring while describing a surveillance nightmare.

jokes.text: December 18, 2015. That was the opening day of Star Wars: Episode VII. You were muttering that date in your sleep for months.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining how Leslie missed the contract revision
jokes.punchline: Leslie's obsessive Star Wars anticipation was exploited
jokes.explanation: Great character callback. Leslie's nerd obsession being weaponized against her is both funny and believable.

jokes.text: Is Star Wars the one with the little wizard boy?
jokes.setup: Discussion of Star Wars date
jokes.punchline: Ron's complete ignorance of pop culture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment. Confusing Star Wars with Harry Potter while maintaining complete confidence is exactly right.

jokes.text: Kung-Fu Billy. Jeremy Punch-Guy. Ed Kick.
jokes.setup: Manager suggesting alternative character names
jokes.punchline: Increasingly lazy action hero names
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from normal to absurd. 'Ed Kick' is perfectly lazy while 'Jeremy Punch-Guy' is oddly specific.

jokes.text: I once sat in the original Batmobile. James Woods follows my niece on Twitter.
jokes.setup: Manager listing his credentials
jokes.punchline: Increasingly pathetic attempts at name-dropping
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of desperate credentials. The James Woods detail is perfectly weird and pathetic.

jokes.text: Les Vegetables.
jokes.setup: Ron admitting he used a fake name for his steakhouse card
jokes.punchline: The hilariously obvious fake name choice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ron moment. Using 'Les Vegetables' at a steakhouse is perfectly absurd and character-appropriate paranoia.

jokes.text: You'd be a checkout girl at a gas station if it wasn't for that internship.
jokes.setup: Leslie defending the value of the Parks internship to April
jokes.punchline: Blunt assessment of April's alternative career path
jokes.explanation: Leslie's characteristic bluntness when defending her department. The specificity of 'checkout girl at a gas station' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Sweet potato pie, unlikely animal friend pairings, Jennifer Love Hewitt.
jokes.setup: April using her anger management technique again
jokes.punchline: Different set of random pleasant things, including Jennifer Love Hewitt
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to earlier technique. Jennifer Love Hewitt is a perfectly random celebrity choice for April's list.

jokes.text: I think there are certain questions about certain aspects of certain parts of this situation that require an evaluation about whether or not this is or is not a valid argument.
jokes.setup: Perd attempting to sound judicial
jokes.punchline: Completely meaningless word salad that says nothing
jokes.explanation: Peak Perd - using many words to say absolutely nothing. The repetitive structure makes the emptiness funnier.

jokes.text: Whoa! That's a serious allegaysh, homie.
jokes.setup: Ben making serious accusation about data mining
jokes.punchline: Roscoe's continued casual tech-bro speak for serious topics
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency. Roscoe can't help speaking in slang even when responding to serious accusations.

jokes.text: To be honest, Judge Perd is stumped by this case. I've also misplaced my judge hammer.
jokes.setup: Perd admitting he can't handle the case
jokes.punchline: Also admitting he's lost his gavel (calling it a 'hammer')
jokes.explanation: Perfect Perd incompetence. Calling a gavel a 'judge hammer' while admitting he's lost it is wonderfully absurd.

jokes.text: Get this--they're gonna pay me the minimum wage.
jokes.setup: Andy announcing his new janitor job as good news
jokes.punchline: Being excited about minimum wage after his TV show success
jokes.explanation: Pure Andy optimism. He's genuinely excited about minimum wage, showing his complete lack of business sense.

jokes.text: Do you want to eat at my restaurant for free? Every week, you could come once a week, and it's free.
jokes.setup: Tom trying to convince station manager to give Andy rights
jokes.punchline: Increasingly desperate bribery attempts
jokes.explanation: Tom's desperation is palpable. The repetitive offer shows he's running out of ideas and getting flustered.

jokes.text: You should up your therapy to seven times a week, stop dressing that way, and give me your wallet.
jokes.setup: April deciding she likes telling people what to do
jokes.punchline: Immediate escalation to personal criticism and robbery
jokes.explanation: Perfect April escalation. Going from reasonable advice to personal attacks to theft is wonderfully absurd.

jokes.text: Victor Garber, James Garner, Jennifer Garner.
jokes.setup: April using her therapy technique again
jokes.punchline: Going alphabetical with celebrity names
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing April's system. The Garner connection is a good detail showing her organizational mind.

jokes.text: talk about that shade Nicki Minaj threw at Jesse Eisenberg at the BAFTA awards. No. he didn't! Uh, settle in, because I have some very strong feelings about this.
jokes.setup: Lucy wanting to discuss celebrity drama
jokes.punchline: Tom's passionate investment in celebrity award show politics
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Tom. His immediate strong opinions about celebrity drama fit his personality perfectly.

jokes.text: This is a flying robot I just shot out of the sky after it delivered a package to my house.
jokes.setup: Ron arriving with mysterious object
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of destroying delivery drone
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson moment. Of course he would shoot down a drone. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: so I destroyed the robot. No one is safe from these bastards.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining his motivation for shooting the drone
jokes.punchline: Declaring war on Gryzzl for targeting his son
jokes.explanation: Great character turn. Ron going from neutral to declaring war when his family is threatened is perfectly in character.
